text.title: Voice Implementation Procedure
text.summary: Six-step procedure from latency-budget assessment to deployed WebSocket endpoint with per-turn audit.

step.name: pick-stack
step.action: If budget <300ms and provider acceptable → choose `openai_realtime`. Else choose `stt_llm_tts`.

step.name: pick-vad
step.input: microphone environment (silent booth vs office vs mobile)
step.action: Default Silero (`torch.hub.load('snakers4/silero-vad', 'silero_vad')`). Use WebRTC if torch dependency unwanted.
step.output: `vad` value in config + initialised VAD client
step.decision-gate: energy VAD blocked unless silent booth

step.name: wire-state-machine
step.input: VAD client, STT/Realtime endpoint, LLM client, TTS client
step.action: Build state machine IDLE → LISTENING → PROCESSING → SPEAKING → IDLE with explicit transition guards.

step.name: register-tools
step.action: For each tool: if blocking, wrap call with `asyncio.to_thread`; if async, await directly. Reject mixing sync registration with async tools.
step.output: tool dispatcher with explicit executor mode
step.decision-gate: any registered tool must have a declared execution mode

step.name: post-process-output
step.action: Strip markdown (`re.sub(r'[*_`#>]', '', text)`); cap output to `max_response_tokens`. Apply sliding window to history before next turn.

step.name: wrap-ws-endpoint
step.action: Mount `/ws/voice` endpoint. Catch `WebSocketDisconnect` separately; on every turn write audit row with all 5 fields.
step.output: FastAPI WebSocket route + audit log writer
step.decision-gate: audit row must contain all 5 fields or the turn is rejected
